name: Ziltivekimab reduced IL-6-linked inflammatory and thrombotic biomarkers
summary: RESCUE provides direct human target-engagement support for IL-6 ligand inhibition in a high-risk chronic inflammation population.
statement: In chronic kidney disease patients with elevated hsCRP, ziltivekimab produced dose-dependent reductions in hsCRP and other inflammatory or thrombotic biomarkers over 12 to 24 weeks.
population_or_model: Adults with moderate to severe chronic kidney disease and hsCRP of at least 2 mg/L
time_horizon: 12-week primary endpoint with 24-week biomarker and safety follow-up
quantitative_note: Median hsCRP fell 77%, 88%, and 92% in the 7.5 mg, 15 mg, and 30 mg groups versus 4% with placebo at 12 weeks.
caveats: RESCUE measured biomarkers, not cardiovascular events or aging outcomes.
The trial population had chronic kidney disease and elevated inflammation.
Outcome evidence depends on larger phase 3 results.
